what_it_does: An empty Git placeholder file that ensures the store/ directory is tracked by version control even when it contains no other files.
conventions: Follows the common Git convention of using a .gitkeep file to preserve an otherwise empty directory in a repository
notes: Empty .gitkeep placeholder that keeps the store directory under version control.
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of tracking an empty directory matches the actual empty .gitkeep file.
intention_signals: Preserve the store/ directory in the repository so it remains available for runtime, build, or deployment use even when empty.
contribution: Ensures the store/ directory is not pruned by Git, providing a stable filesystem path for downstream processes.
summary_for_embedding: The file company/store/.gitkeep is an empty Git placeholder that keeps the store directory tracked in version control.
intention_for_embedding: Ensure the store directory is retained in the repository even when it has no other contents.
